containerNetoffEquivalent.notes: Replay reused already provisioned public source files and reran deterministic source split leakage checks without download calls.

replay.divergence: Replay preserved the Batch 25 leakage interpretation: no confirmed duplicate/group leakage mechanism was found for the executed deltas.

result.interpretation: Leakage was not automatically inferred from split delta; not-testable group fields and class/protocol difficulty remain visible.

targets.name: UCI HAR Smartphones
targets.prior_evidence: Batches 13, 14, 21, and 22 showed a source-vs-random macro-F1 delta around the moderate split-risk range; subject_train and subject_test are available.
targets.safety_note: Public safe wearable-sensor classification; no medical advice claim.
targets.why_selected: It has explicit subject IDs, so group/subject leakage can be tested rather than guessed.

targets.name: UCI Statlog Shuttle
targets.prior_evidence: Batches 14 and 15 found high split-risk symptoms and rare-class / metric-risk limits on the source split.
targets.why_selected: It is the strongest prior split-risk target and tests whether leakage is actually found or class imbalance better explains the delta.

targets.name: UCI Statlog Landsat Satellite
targets.prior_evidence: Batches 14, 16, and 20 preserved Landsat as spatial/file/protocol-risk evidence with missing spatial-coordinate limitations.
targets.why_selected: It anchors source-protocol split evidence but is held for Batch 26 because spatial groups are absent from the public table.

targets.name: UCI Letter Recognition
targets.prior_evidence: Batches 14, 21, and 22 treated Letter as an approximated row-order protocol and a near-control with low source-vs-random delta.
targets.why_selected: It gives Batch 25 a low-risk/near-control target and tests whether leakage tools overclaim on an easy benchmark.

targets.name: UCI Vehicle Silhouettes
targets.prior_evidence: Batches 22 and 23 selected and downgraded Vehicle as an ambiguous shard-layout target with no source-declared canonical split.
targets.why_selected: It keeps the required ambiguous/protocol-weak case visible without forcing a false leakage conclusion.
